A tribute with love

To the Woman
Who Built Our World

From the Philippines to Japan to the United States, every mile traveled was love in action.

Scroll

From your children

You gave everything so we could have something.

There is a kind of love that doesn't announce itself. It wakes before the sun, boards a plane across the ocean, and choose to be selfless, because for the family it carries in its heart.

You built a house from nothing. You built a life from sacrifice. And without ever making it about you, you made us possible.

You asked for Love and Peace for your birthday. We hope this small corner of the internet gives you both and reminds you that everything you fought for was worth it.

We are your greatest work. And we are so, so proud to be yours.

Happy Birthday, Mama.

Memories

A Life in Photographs

Words from us to you

What We Want You to Know

Thank you ma for loving me, Sobrang Thankful ako mama na ikaw ang naging mama ko. Sobrang proud ako sayo, I don't know how you carried all of that, but I know it made me who I am. I love you more than any word I know.

Jose Christopher A. Ojano

Every time I think about how far you've gone, literally across the world, just for us, I feel both humbled and so incredibly proud. You are my definition of strength. Happy Birthday, Mama.

Joanna Audrey A. Ojano

Happy Birthday Mama! I wish all the best. I miss you so much and I hope you are happily celebrating your birthday even though we are apart. Thank you for spoiling us whenever you can and sana mag tira ka rin for yourself. Happy birthday and Godbless.

John Adrian A. Ojano

With all the love in the world

Happy Birthday,
Mama.

You asked for love and peace. We hope you feel both tonight, wherever in the world you are. You are never far from our hearts.

John Adrian A. Ojano Bunso
Joanna Audrey A. Ojano Middle Child
Jose Christopher A. Ojano 1st Born
